cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.21)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Lodestone - Shared SKSE framework (Phase 16)
# Stage A: validation plugin. Just writes a line to the SKSE log.
# Stage B: per-module infrastructure + Papyrus API (Lodestone.GetVersion).
# Stage C.1 Part 1.5: CastTime module, TRACE ONLY (logs, changes nothing).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the DLL version.
# These numbers feed three consumers:
#   1. The SKSE plugin version boilerplate (generated by add_commonlibsse_plugin)
#   2. The load log line
#   3. Lodestone.GetVersion() / GetVersionString() on the Papyrus side (via Version.h)
# DLL version - INDEPENDENT of the mod version on Nexus.
project(
    Lodestone
    VERSION 1.5.0
    LANGUAGES CXX
)

# CommonLibSSE-NG requires C++23.
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 23)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Version.h generated from src/Version.h.in (Stage B.1).
# @ONLY: only expands @VAR@, ignores ${VAR} - keeps CMake from touching C++ code.
# Generated file goes to the binary dir (build artifact, not versioned).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
configure_file(
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/src/Version.h.in"
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated/Version.h"
    @ONLY
)

# Resolved via the vcpkg port (colorglass registry, see vcpkg-configuration.json).
find_package(CommonLibSSE CONFIG REQUIRED)

# SafetyHook - inline detours for NON-VIRTUAL engine functions.
#
# WHY A SECOND HOOKING MECHANISM EXISTS. CommonLibSSE's Trampoline::write_branch
# does not detour a function body: it assumes the address already holds a 5-byte
# rel32 branch, decodes that displacement, and returns the branch's original
# target. It is a tool for redirecting an existing CALL SITE. Pointed at a
# prologue it returns an address in no loaded module, and the first call through
# it crashes the game - which is exactly what happened here before this was
# understood.
#
# Detouring a function body requires relocating the displaced prologue, which
# requires knowing instruction boundaries and rewriting rip-relative operands.
# That is disassembler work; SafetyHook does it (via Zydis) and additionally
# suspends other threads while patching, which matters in a game with a render
# thread and a script VM running concurrently.
#
# THE RULE, so this does not drift:
#   virtual function      -> vtable swap (REL::Relocation::write_vfunc)
#   non-virtual function  -> SafetyHook inline hook
#   Trampoline::write_branch -> NOT USED. Only valid on a call site, and every
#                               use of it in this project was on a function body.
#
# PINNED BY OVERRIDE in vcpkg.json, not by the baseline: safetyhook is absent
# from the pinned builtin-baseline, and moving that baseline would re-resolve
# every other dependency of a project that builds clean. The override keeps the
# rest of the dependency set frozen - which also means bumping the baseline will
# NOT bump safetyhook. That is deliberate; change it in vcpkg.json explicitly.
find_package(safetyhook CONFIG REQUIRED)

# add_commonlibsse_plugin creates the DLL target, wires up CommonLibSSE linkage,
# and auto-generates the SKSE version boilerplate (SKSEPlugin_Version).
# Output file will be Lodestone.dll.
add_commonlibsse_plugin(
    ${PROJECT_NAME}
    AUTHOR "Awrawra"
    SOURCES
        src/plugin.cpp
        src/Core/Log.cpp
        src/Core/Papyrus.cpp
        src/Core/PluginInfo.cpp
        src/Core/CastTime.cpp
        src/Core/BookFramework.cpp
        src/Core/SpellTomes.cpp
        src/Core/MagicScaling.cpp
)

target_compile_features(${PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE cxx_std_23)

target_link_libraries(${PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE safetyhook::safetyhook)

# Where the generated Version.h is found by #include "Version.h".
target_include_directories(${PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated"
)

# PCH.h is mandatory for CommonLibSSE-NG. Force-included in every file of the
# target, it brings in the CommonLib headers before Relocation.h gets parsed.
# add_commonlibsse_plugin already applies /Zc:preprocessor and other required flags.
target_precompile_headers(${PROJECT_NAME} PRIVATE src/PCH.h)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Copies the DLL to the MO2 deploy mod after each build (Stage B).
#
# The deploy folder is NOT a worktree of anything - it's only a destination for
# the compiled artifact, outside git. Source lives in this repo; the compiled
# binary lives there. Never the other way around.
#
# Enabled in Stage B to remove manual copying: forgetting to copy makes the
# game load the stale DLL, and the resulting bug looks like a code issue.
#
# LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER is overridable: pass -DLODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER=... on
# the CMake command line, or set it in the environment. A contributor's deploy
# path is not ours to hardcode, so the default below is only a fallback used
# when neither is provided. Set it to an empty string to skip deploy entirely.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(NOT DEFINED LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER AND DEFINED ENV{LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER})
    set(LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER "$ENV{LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER}")
endif()

if(NOT DEFINED LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER)
    set(LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER "G:/Modlists/Skyrim Dev/mods/Lodestone - DEV/SKSE/Plugins")
endif()

if(LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER)
    message(STATUS "DLL deploy folder: ${LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER}")
    add_custom_command(
        TARGET ${PROJECT_NAME}
        POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E make_directory "${LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER}"
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "$<TARGET_FILE:${PROJECT_NAME}>" "${LODESTONE_OUTPUT_FOLDER}"
        VERBATIM
    )
endif()
